mappings.value: The time derivative of rho plus the theta derivative of the phase-velocity field times rho equals zero. Here rho of theta omega t is the continuum phase density, omega plus K r sine of psi minus theta is the local phase velocity, and r and psi are the collective order-parameter variables.

Equation 7 is the continuity equation for the infinite-population density. It says oscillators are transported around the phase circle by the mean-field velocity, but probability is neither created nor destroyed.
This is the continuum analogue of the microscopic dynamics. Once rho is introduced, the Kuramoto model becomes a transport problem for a density instead of only a system of many coupled ODEs.
Use Eq. 7 when N is large and you want to reason with a density over phase and frequency. It is the governing equation that later stationary and self-consistency formulas are built from.
